text: Big Bend National Park
zh: 大弯国家公园

text: vast Chihuahuan Desert, largest desert in North America,
zh: 广袤的奇瓦瓦沙漠是北美洲最大、海拔最高的沙漠,

text: and the highest in elevation, reaches a thousand miles from the heart
zh: 从古老墨西哥的腹地延伸约一千英里,

text: of Old Mexico into the southwestern United States.
zh: 一直延伸到美国西南部。

text: Here in far west Texas, on the Mexican border, there is a landmass larger than the state of Rhode Island, isolated from civilization.
zh: 在得克萨斯州最西端、毗邻墨西哥边境的地方,有一片比罗德岛州还大的土地,与文明世界隔绝。

text: Big Bend National Park protects more than 5 ,000 species of living things.
zh: 大弯国家公园保护着五千多种生物。

text: A river, the Rio Grande.
zh: 一条河流,格兰德河。

text: Loupes from the Rockies in Colorado to the Gulf of Mexico.
zh: 它从科罗拉多州的落基山脉蜿蜒流向墨西哥湾。

text: Its flow bending through the desert tracts of Texas.
zh: 它的水流在得克萨斯州的沙漠地带蜿蜒曲折。

text: Texans call it Big Bend Country.
zh: 得克萨斯人称这里为“大弯地区”。

text: When Spanish explorers first saw it some centuries ago, They called it despoblado,
zh: 几个世纪前,西班牙探险者初见此地时,称它为“despoblado”,

text: the uninhabited land. They were wrong.
zh: 意为无人居住之地。他们错了。

text: Today, it is the home of Big Bend National Park.
zh: 如今,这里是大弯国家公园的所在地。

text: Here in far west Texas, near the Mexican border, this remote park is larger than the state of Rhode Island.
zh: 在得克萨斯州最西端、靠近墨西哥边境的地方,这座偏远的公园面积比罗德岛州还大。

text: And it is inhabited. inhabited by more than 5 ,000 species of living things,
zh: 而且这里确实有生命居住,栖息着五千多种生物,

text: not counting visitors who find the park very much alive with things to see and do.
zh: 这还不包括那些发现公园处处充满生机、值得游览体验的游客。

text: river bends around the Chisos Mountains.
zh: 河流环绕着奇索斯山脉蜿蜒而过。

text: Atop these peaks grow forests. Trees which hold moisture and give shade from the punishing sun.
zh: 山顶生长着森林,树木锁住水汽,遮挡毒辣的阳光。

text: Mountain slopes made white with winter snows.
zh: 冬雪覆盖下,山坡一片洁白。

text: Desert, yes. But there are also desert grasslands.
zh: 这里是沙漠,没错,但也有沙漠草原。

text: And desert flowers. And
zh: 还有沙漠中的花朵。

text: there's Water in the flow of the Rio Grande.
zh: 格兰德河的水流带来了生机。

text: For more than a thousand miles, this meandering river is the international boundary between two sovereign nations,
zh: 这条蜿蜒千余英里的河流,是两个主权国家之间的国界线,

text: the USA and the Republic of Mexico.
zh: 即美国与墨西哥合众国之间的边界。

text: Big Bend National Park administers 244 miles of that boundary.
zh: 大弯国家公园管辖着这条边界线中的244英里。

text: This border has not always been inviolate.
zh: 这条边界并非一直安然无恙。

text: Rustlers have raided here and bandits looted.
zh: 盗贼曾在此劫掠,土匪也曾肆意抢夺。

text: When pursued, they simply fled to Mexico across the river.
zh: 一旦被追捕,他们便径直渡河逃往墨西哥。

text: One notable outlaw was Dorteo Arango, a volatile mixture of Robin Hood and Bandito,
zh: 其中一位臭名昭著的亡命之徒名叫多特奥·阿兰戈,他是侠盗与匪徒的混合体,

text: who was also known on both sides of the U .S.-Mexican border as Pancho Villa.
zh: 在美墨边境两侧,他更广为人知的名字是“潘乔·维拉”。

text: He was a brilliant horseman, marksman, and general.
zh: 他是一位杰出的骑手、神枪手和将军。

text: Because of changes in elevation, From cool mountainsides
zh: 由于海拔变化,从凉爽的山坡

text: to dry, hot desert, the park abounds in different kinds of wildlife.
zh: 到干燥炎热的沙漠,公园内野生动物种类繁多。

text: But most creatures withdraw into the shadows during the day, particularly in the desert.
zh: 但白天大多数动物都躲藏在阴影中,尤其是在沙漠地带。

text: At night, they become wildly alive and forage for food.
zh: 到了夜晚,它们才活跃起来,四处觅食。

text: lizards, snakes, tortoises and turtles, and peccaries.
zh: 包括蜥蜴、蛇类、陆龟、水龟以及野猪。

text: With over 450 species in the park, birdwatching is a popular pursuit.
zh: 公园内有450多种鸟类,观鸟成为一项热门活动。

text: Big Bend is one of the largest, most remote national parks in the lower 48 states,
zh: 大弯国家公园是美国本土48州中最大、最偏远的国家公园之一,

text: which makes hiking there a real adventure. On trails blazed by landmarks,
zh: 这让徒步旅行成为一场真正的冒险,沿途有著名地标指引方向,

text: such as these two stone towers, nicknamed Mule Ears.
zh: 比如这两座石塔,被昵称为“骡耳”。

text: Grapevine Hills Trail, an easy walk, leads to a group of balanced rocks.
zh: 葡萄藤山径是一条轻松易走的小路,通向一组平衡奇石。

text: After a hike, tired adventurers can soak off fatigue in the naturally therapeutic waters of Big Bend's own hot springs.
zh: 徒步之后,疲惫的探险者可以在大弯公园天然的温泉水中舒缓疲劳。

text: 118 miles of river ribbons the southern boundary of the park.
zh: 118英里长的河流蜿蜒构成公园的南部边界。

text: A joy to visitors who want their canoeing skills challenged by paddling through spectacular Santa Elena Canyon.
zh: 想挑战独木舟技巧的游客,可以在壮丽的圣埃琳娜峡谷中划船,尽享其中乐趣。

text: Sheer rock cliffs rise 1400 feet above the water.
zh: 陡峭的岩壁高耸于水面之上,高达1400英尺。

text: one of the sights that places Big Bend among the most spectacular outdoor areas in North America.
zh: 这正是大弯国家公园跻身北美最壮丽户外胜地之列的景观之一。
